values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

Patch Set 1:

(1 comment)

Does this require specifying the exact IP address, or is it possible to specify a subnet (either with an explicit mask, or implicitly using the broadcast address for a subnet like 192.168.10.0 or 192.168.10.255?

That would make it much more useful for configuring a large number of nodes at once, like ip2net, rather than having to run a different command on every node.  I'm trying to avoid situations where the LNet config has to be customized for each of thousands of nodes, rather than having a single config for all of them.  

Frank, weren't you also working on something similar?

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Passed enforced test review-ldiskfs-dne-arm on RHEL 8.8/aarch64, RHEL 8.8/x86_64 uploaded by Onyx Autotest 2 from onyx-91vm3: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/fe3ce636-9633-4d19-9de5-26a16a35c569 ran 5 tests.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> (1 comment)
> 
> Does this require specifying the exact IP address, or is it possible to specify a subnet (either with an explicit mask, or implicitly using the broadcast address for a subnet like 192.168.10.0 or 192.168.10.255?
> 
> That would make it much more useful for configuring a large number of nodes at once, like ip2net, rather than having to run a different command on every node.  I'm trying to avoid situations where the LNet config has to be customized for each of thousands of nodes, rather than having a single config for all of them.  

I thought about implementing that approach not with just network addresses but device interfaces as well. That would require changing the behavior of NI setup tho. Currently trying to set up a non existant device interface causes a failure. We have to silently ignore setup failures otherwise unless all interfaces or addresses fail.

> Frank, weren't you also working on something similar?

He has a year + old patch. I just did this patch since its one approach we can take to doing IPv6 testing. This way we can pick a IPv6 address directly instead of defaulting to IPv4 address.

Patch Set 1:

Yes I was, but the original problem seemed to be in ipnets.I had a version that added a single ip address, but it didn't pass review because of the syntax. Still working n the ipnets bug though.

Patch Set 1:

Oh and I implemented the parts on the module side to set the correct nid.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

I am not sure if this is really related to LU-13642 because the originating ticket was base on a ipnets problem.
I think we definitely need a test case for this.

parents.subject: LU-16796 lfsck: Change lfsck_layout_slave_target to use kref

commit.subject: LU-13642 lnet: Allow dynamic IP specification
commit.message:
LU-13642 lnet: Allow dynamic IP specification

Currently you can setup an NI only using the device interface.
It is possible that a device interface has more than one IP
address. This change updates lnet_net_cmd() to setup an NI using
a specific network address.

For further reference please read

IP specification in LNet
https://wiki.whamcloud.com/display/LNet/IP+specification+in+LNet

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity-lnet
Change-Id: I2c456790fe9534bbfe02b0330cce73e80318cc1c
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <jsimmons@infradead.org>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/53605
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Chris Horn <chris.horn@hpe.com>
Reviewed-by: Frank Sehr <fsehr@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
